title: macOS Sequoia Beta 2 Adds Support for iPhone Mirroring
content:
With the second developer beta of macOS Sequoia, Apple has added support for iPhone Mirroring, one of the main updates coming to the Mac. ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring is a Continuity feature that lets you control your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå from your Mac.


When you're signed in to the same Apple ID on a Mac and an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå, you can use ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring to interact with your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå even when the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå is locked. You can open up and use apps, deal with notifications, send messages, and more.

You can use your Mac keyboard, trackpad, or mouse with your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå, which is useful for typing up long emails and other documents on the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå, and it provides an easy way to keep up with your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå notifications without having to pull out your device and check it. When you click on a notification on your Mac when using ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring, it is supposed to open up right into the app on your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå.

Though it isn't enabled in this beta, ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring will eventually allow files, photos, and videos to be dragged and dropped between your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå and Mac and vice versa. Apple plans to add that ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring feature later in 2024, and it won't be available when ‚ÄåmacOS Sequoia‚Äå first launches.


‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring requires an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå running iOS 18 beta 2 and a Mac running ‚ÄåmacOS Sequoia‚Äå beta 2, and it is accessible using the new ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring icon in the Dock. It works on Macs that have an Apple silicon chip or a T2 Security Chip, and you'll need to have the devices near each other with Bluetooth and Wi-Fi and you'll need to be signed in to the same ‚ÄåApple ID‚Äå with two-factor authentication turned on. ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring can't be used when Sidecar or AirPlay are activated.

Today's second beta also adds ‚ÄåiOS 18‚Äå and iPadOS 18 support for remote control when using the SharePlay screen sharing feature. This option allows you to ask for permission to control someone's device remotely so that you can provide more direct technical assistance for problem solving.

The ‚ÄåmacOS Sequoia‚Äå and ‚ÄåiOS 18‚Äå betas are only available to developers right now, but Apple plans to release a public beta in July that will allow anyone to try out the new ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring feature before it launches. The updates will see a public release this fall.
